what_it_does: Defines a React-style calendar screen with a title, filter/sort/search toolbar, horizontal task reminder cards, month navigation for June 2026, weekday headers, and several hardcoded calendar rows with colored task chips.
observations: The file defines four components: TopTask, CalChip, Day, and CalendarScreen.
CalendarScreen is assigned to window.CalendarScreen rather than exported with module syntax.
All visible calendar data is hardcoded, including task names, due dates, placeholder descriptions, month label, and day numbers.
Toolbar controls are rendered without event handlers or local state.
The search field has a small inline SVG icon and a placeholder only.
Task chips use kind-based class names and include a dot element before the label.
Day supports muted and today visual states through conditional class names.
Several inline styles are used for the Manage label and Task Type button.
standouts: The screen is structured as a static design-system calendar mock with reusable local fragments for task cards, chips, and day cells.
The calendar combines a reminder strip above a month grid in the same screen.
Color classification is represented through CSS class suffixes rather than data objects or style maps.
conventions: Uses function components with JSX and simple prop destructuring.
Uses BEM-like cv-* class names for screen, toolbar, calendar, buttons, chips, and task elements.
Uses conditional class-name interpolation directly in JSX.
Uses placeholder content repeatedly to represent design-system states.
Mixes class-based styling with a small amount of inline styling.
concerns: Controls labeled Filter, Sort, Clear Filters, Manage, month arrows, and Task Type have no behavior in this file.
Calendar data and task content are fixed literals, so the rendered month is not data-driven here.
The Day component wraps cells in an unclassified div, leaving cell-level styling dependent on surrounding layout assumptions.
Emoji and text are used together inside the Task Type control, which may rely on font/platform rendering differences.
notes: This file is a static calendar screen assembly for a platform UI kit, emphasizing visual layout and state examples over runtime behavior.
questions: Should the toolbar controls eventually connect to filtering, sorting, search, and task-type selection state?
Should the month navigation arrows update the displayed month?
Is the calendar intentionally partial, ending after June 16, or is the remaining month omitted for handoff brevity?
What values map to the chip kind color classes beyond the examples shown here?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and screen title indicate a calendar screen, and the file renders a calendar-style screen with tasks, navigation, and date grid content.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to show how a calendar screen should look in the platform UI kit, including toolbar controls, reminder cards, month navigation, muted dates, current-day styling, and colored task markers.
novelty: The file combines a horizontal reminder-card strip with a compact month grid and color-coded inline task chips in one static screen.
contribution: Contributes a calendar screen substrate with local reusable pieces for reminder cards, calendar chips, and day labels, plus concrete visual states for muted dates, current date highlighting, and colored task categories.
summary_for_embedding: A React-style Calendar.jsx screen that statically renders a platform calendar UI with toolbar actions, search, reminder cards, June 2026 month navigation, weekday headers, day cells, and colored task chips.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting a design-system calendar screen layout with representative task, date, and category states rather than implementing interactive scheduling behavior.

symbols.does: Renders a small task reminder card with a colored name, fixed due-date label, and placeholder description.
symbols.description: TopTask takes color and name props, applies the color as an additional class on the task name, and renders fixed surrounding copy. It is used to populate the horizontal Top Tasks & Reminders row with repeated visual examples.

symbols.does: Renders a colored calendar task chip with a dot and label.
symbols.description: CalChip takes kind and label props, adds the kind as a class on the chip, and includes an inner dot element before the text. It provides the repeated in-day task marker used inside calendar cells.

symbols.does: Renders a calendar day number with optional muted and today classes plus child content.
symbols.description: Day accepts n, muted, today, and children props, then builds the day-number class string from the boolean state props. It acts as the cell content wrapper for plain dates and dates containing one or more CalChip components.

symbols.does: Composes the full calendar screen from title, toolbar, reminder cards, month navigation, weekday headers, and hardcoded week rows.
symbols.description: CalendarScreen assembles all local components into a static screen for June 2026. It includes non-wired toolbar buttons and search input, a reminder row of TopTask instances, and a calendar grid with muted previous-month dates, a today marker, and colored task chips.
